notes: SHIP v1.14.217 — RACK-TEMPLATE LIBRARY, Stage 2a (per HANDOFF-rack-templates.md / (1), base .216). Makes the Rack Map floor platform-aware: instead of a flat generic 24U rail, it renders the real rack geometry for the loaded Master's platform. SCOPE — this is 2a (floor geometry + templates + kind CSS), self-contained + additive. DEFERRED to 2b (folds into the node-status Part A/B1 ship): touch-point 2 (elevation template fallback when a real rack lacks slot data) and the MASTER SLOT U-POSITIONS ingest validation (uStart/uEnd populate + unplaced/overlap lists) — 2b overlaps the node-status Master merge, so it ships there. WHAT LANDED: (1) RACK_TEMPLATES const library keyed by platform id (gb300-nvl72, gb200-nvl72, hgx-b200-dlc, hgx-h200, hgx-h100) — store-less reference skeletons, frameU + ordered top->bottom zones {kind,label,u,count}; NVL72 = 18 compute + 9 NVSwitch + 6 power shelf (firm across NVIDIA/HPE/SemiAnalysis sources), canonical NVIDIA reference order (centered switch spine). (2) rackTemplate_keyFor(str) — maps any platform/host-model string to a template key; order gb300/gb200 BEFORE b200 so 'gb200' can't match the 'b200' branch. (3) rackViewer_resolvePlatformKey() — resolves the floor platform FROM DATA ONLY, never a hardcoded default: active deployment rack.platform (deploy_getActive) -> derived site-profile platforms (siteProfile_load().platforms) -> dominant platform in the loaded Master (window._lastPhantomMaster racksByCab rack.platform / hosts[].model, tallied) -> null. Every hop typeof-guarded, so a missing accessor or no Master falls through to the generic rail. (4) rackViewer_renderFloor(target, platformId) extended: when platformId resolves to a RACK_TEMPLATES entry, render _rackViewer_templateFloorHtml (labeled zone bands, kind-tinted, height ∝ u×count, frame header 'REFERENCE · <label> · <cooling> · ~<kW>kW · <frameU>U' + note); otherwise the EXISTING generic 24U cage-nut rail (unchanged, preserved as the no-Master/unmapped fallback). (5) EDIT-1 call site now passes rackViewer_resolvePlatformKey(). (6) CSS: additive .rv-tpl-frame/.rv-tpl-band/.rv-tpl-band-lbl/.rv-tpl-band-u/.rv-tpl-note + kind band tints on .rv-tpl-band.cn-u-{switch|power|cdu|compute|net|manifold|blank}; existing .cn-u-equipment/.cn-u-context NOT touched (Gate 4). DEVIATIONS FROM SPEC (reported per role): (a) kind tints reuse the app's existing tokens (--vio, --gold, --cyan, --slate) instead of introducing the spec's near-duplicate --violet #9b59ff / --gold #ffd60a — avoids token proliferation; hexes differ slightly but stay in the app's design system. (b) Added cn-u-compute/cn-u-net/cn-u-blank tints alongside the spec's named four (switch/power/cdu/manifold) so every floor band kind has a color. (c) Floor renders zone bands as a single stacked frame (skeleton), not a twin-rail per-U elevation — the twin-rail per-U numbering belongs to the real-rack elevation (2b). SW CACHE_VERSION bumped .216->.217: stamp-only, no precache LIST change, REQUIRED so devices fetch the new HTML (same no-op-without-bump gotcha as prior ships). Gates: node --check sw.js + 4 inline HTML blocks · CSS braces net-0 delta on modified rule but +N balanced new rules (all balanced) · CRLF preserved · RACK_TEMPLATES zone sums sane vs frameU (NVL72 core 35U placed + 12U manifold = 47 <= 48) · three-stamp lockstep .216->.217. ROLLBACK: git revert — additive (1 const, 4 fns, N CSS rules, 1 signature+callsite change); no store writes, no schema/migration; generic rail restored on revert. DEVICE-VERIFY (hard stop): remove+re-add PWA, let new SW activate, confirm app reads v1.14.217, then: (1) NO MASTER — cold start / nothing loaded, open Rack Map -> generic cage-nut rail (twin L/R, U24->U1), NOT a platform template, NOT blank; (2) MASTER-DRIVEN PLATFORM — load a Master whose racks are HGX H200 -> floor draws the hgx-h200 skeleton (42U, leaf pair + 8U node bands, cyan compute); load a Master with GB300 racks -> gb300-nvl72 skeleton (48U, centered violet NVLink-switch spine, cyan compute banks above/below, gold power shelves at extremities, dim manifold zone). Confirms platform is READ FROM DATA, never hardcoded; (3) MASTER OVERRIDE unchanged — opening a real rack still renders its live elevation (renderElevation), template only the cold-start floor; (4) band legibility — switch=violet, power/cdu=gold, compute=cyan, manifold/blank=dim, each labeled with '×count' and its U span; (5) LEGACY ?legacy=1 byte-identical. If a Master is loaded but the floor stays generic -> platform didn't resolve (rack.platform / host model didn't match a template key, or site profile not derived) — check rackViewer_resolvePlatformKey against the Master's actual platform strings. If blank after SW-looking change -> cache-key bump didn't take.
